title: Causal rolling degradation acceleration
summary: Recent research frames tyre degradation as a latent stochastic process with pit-stop resets, while current solutions mostly use static ratios and same-lap medians.
rationale: Recent research frames tyre degradation as a latent stochastic process with pit-stop resets, while current solutions mostly use static ratios and same-lap medians. The next-lap pit decision should react to short-term worsening, not just absolute tyre age.
implementation_hint: Sort by Race, Driver, Stint, LapNumber and compute lag-only rolling 2/3/5-lap slopes and EWMs for LapTime_Delta, LapTime (s), Cumulative_Degradation, Position_Change, plus acceleration terms. Reset windows at Stint changes and fill first-lap values conservatively.
expected_effect: Adds temporal evidence of a tyre cliff and should diversify from static GBM features.
risk: Random row order must not be trusted; rolling features must never include current target or future laps.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
